behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (manifest parser/resolver -> vulnerability-check input), not one pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real package manager execution or OS events. Candidate tests: (A) Integration setup: wire manifest parser + transitive resolver + query-prep with fixture manifest/lockfile; assertion: query input includes every direct+transitive dep; catches dropped transitive nodes. (B) Unit setup: test resolver function alone; assertion: returns expected list; misses wiring bugs where resolver output is not propagated. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior says dependencies are extracted from manifest and included in check, but does not require real subprocess/network/OS.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser+transitive-resolution+check-input assembly in-process on a fixture project and assert exact dependency set (direct + transitive) reaches vulnerability-check stage.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between query builder/version-range matcher and API client/response handling. REAL-INFRA: real HTTP is not required to catch this; a stubbed DB API can validate sent range and returned matches. Candidate tests: (A) Integration setup: scanner wired to stub vulnerability API with package having multiple vulnerable ranges; assertion: only CVEs matching manifest range are retained; catches name-only matching bug. (B) Unit setup: test range comparator only; assertion: comparator true/false; misses wrong request construction or response-filter wiring. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior requires exact range matching logic, not real network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process wiring with a stub vulnerability API that returns mixed version-range CVEs; assert scanner keeps only entries matching manifest constraints.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure ordering logic on local data; no module handoff or I/O required. Candidate tests: (A) Unit setup: list of CVEs with mixed severities/CVSS; assertion: sorted order matches severity priority then CVSS desc; catches comparator mistakes. (B) Integration setup: run full scan pipeline and inspect report order; catches same bug but not needed for minimum failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Test sorting function directly with crafted CVE fixtures to verify severity precedence and CVSS tie-break ordering.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Likely failure appears when parser/editor/serializer are combined: unrelated entries can drift during write-back. Candidate tests: (A) Integration setup: parse real manifest fixture, apply bump, serialize; assertion: semantic diff contains exactly one dependency version change; catches accidental rewrites of other entries. (B) Unit setup: test version-replace helper; assertion: target field updated; misses serialization-layer side effects. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior does not require OS-specific file watchers or external services.
behaviors.test_description: Wire manifest read/parse/update/serialize path in-process and assert only the targeted dependency entry changes in resulting manifest content.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: behavior explicitly says real repository; mocked git/PR API can pass while auth/branch/diff/push/PR creation fail in reality. Candidate tests: (A) System setup: run scanner against a real test repo and real hosting API credentials; assertion: PR exists and diff contains only one dependency bump; catches real git/API integration failures. (B) Integration setup: mock PR client and git wrapper; assertion: createPR called with expected patch; misses remote permission/branching/diff normalization failures. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration due explicit text 'created on the real repository'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end PR creation against an actual sandbox repository and verify opened PR diff changes exactly one dependency version.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Single semver classification rule; no I/O/module boundary required. Candidate tests: (A) Unit setup: feed version pairs (1.2.3->2.0.0, 1.2.3->1.3.0); assertion: major bump flagged true, non-major false; catches semver comparison bugs. (B) Integration setup: run scanner flow and inspect flag; unnecessary for minimum boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparison logic directly with representative version transitions, asserting major-only bumps are marked breaking.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration/state handoff: semver decision must gate PR module and propagate review flag to output module. No real OS/network required to catch gating bug if PR client is spy/stub. Candidate tests: (A) Integration setup: wire breaking-change detector + PR orchestrator + reporter with stub PR backend; assertion: no PR call and review flag present; catches incorrect control flow. (B) Unit setup: test a single 'shouldCreatePR' helper; assertion false on breaking change; misses reporter propagation and module wiring errors. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require validating a real remote PR, only skip + surface flag.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process orchestration with a breaking bump input, assert PR creation path is not invoked and scanner output includes a review-required marker.
